name: BGP-15
summary: BGP-15 is a small-molecule chaperone or heat-shock-support candidate tracked for short human insulin-sensitivity proof-of-concept evidence.
mechanism_summary: Intended to modulate proteostasis and stress-response biology, with the linked human record limited to metabolic biomarker response.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: BGP-15 improved insulin sensitivity in a short proof-of-concept trial; BGP-15 insulin resistance proof-of-concept trial.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
